title: Nuclear safety at risk: what’s changing under Donald Trump
content: As sweeping deregulation accelerates under Donald Trump, long-standing nuclear safety regulations are being rolled back with little public scrutiny, raising new concerns about risks to both human health and the environment.

creator: Kirsten Stade
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: The empty desk policy: Why remote work is the sustainability win we’re ignoring
content: The daily commute is more than an inconvenience; it is a major source of pollution and wasted time. As return-to-office demands grow, remote work may be one of the most overlooked tools for reducing emissions while improving quality of life and accessibility.

creator: Saskia Karges

title: How environmental laws are shifting the focus from humans to nature
content: Environmental laws are evolving from prioritizing human benefits to recognizing nature’s intrinsic rights; this is reflected in the small but growing number of countries that grant legal personhood to natural entities.

creator: Erika Schelby

title: Human Nature Odyssey, Episode 21. Earth Abides (Part 1): Life After Civilization
content: You ever go on a little trip, to just get away from it all — only to come home and find all of civilization collapsed while you were gone and you might be the last person left on earth? Well then you could totally relate to George R. Stewart’s 1949 science-fiction novel, "Earth Abides."

title: Why capitalism relies on nature and care work it does not pay for
content: Modern economies depend on unpriced ecosystem functions and undervalued care and reproductive labor—essential inputs that are difficult to commodify. This tension helps explain environmental degradation, social strain, and the limits of market systems.

creator: Gunnar Rundgren

title: ‘What do we value most?’ Rethinking resilience in a time of polycrisis
content: Professor Jem Bendell proposed the “Deep Adaptation” framework to help people respond to the climate crisis. As global crises intensify, new questions focused on reclamation and regeneration offer ways to navigate an increasingly uncertain world.

title: Crazy Town Episode 122. Birdbrained: When nature and technology collide
content: Jason, Rob, and Asher explore the world of competitive birding, the relationship between those who love nature and the technology they use to connect to it, and how even the most gentle of shared passions can get corrupted by status-fueled competition.

creator: Asher Miller

title: Trump’s ‘God Squad’ pits energy vs. endangered species, but protecting wildlife can be good for business
content: Would you rather have abundant and affordable energy or a clean, healthy planet where wildlife can flourish? It sounds like an either/or choice, but it doesn’t have to be.
snippet: Would you rather have abundant and affordable energy or a clean, healthy planet where wildlife can flourish? It sounds like an either/or choice, but it doesn’t have to be.
creator: Dan Salas

title: Why we’re still marching toward collapse—and how to break the “consensus trance”
content: We continue down a path of collective destruction, in part because of a belief system so pervasive it feels like reality itself. Recognizing our deep interconnectedness is essential to building a more just and sustainable future.
snippet: We continue down a path of collective destruction, in part because of a belief system so pervasive it feels like reality itself. Recognizing our deep interconnectedness is essential to building a more

title: The restorative promise of agroecology: Farming for sovereignty and resilience in Malawi – Part II
content: In Malawi, farmers who have embraced agroecology are navigating the challenges of climate change, market pressures, and community needs.
snippet: In Malawi, farmers who have embraced agroecology are navigating the challenges of climate change, market pressures, and community needs.

title: Truth, lies, and loyalty in the age of Trumpism
content: Why do people cling to falsehoods, even in the face of evidence? Both truth and lies serve social purposes, but holding onto reason becomes essential as we face climate change and rising Trumpism.
snippet: Why do people cling to falsehoods, even in the face of evidence? Both truth and lies serve social purposes, but holding onto reason becomes essential as we face climate change and rising Trumpism.
creator: Richard Heinberg
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: How California’s war on smog and its ambitious car pollution rules made everyone’s air cleaner
content: The Clean Air Act gave California the authority to issue tough pollution standards for vehicles, spurring the development of the catalytic converter and cleaning up America’s air.
snippet: The Clean Air Act gave California the authority to issue tough pollution standards for vehicles, spurring the development of the catalytic converter and cleaning up America’s air.
creator: Ann E. Carlson

title: Climate cracks are spreading — and even the system knows it can’t hold
content: From scientists to intelligence agencies, repeated warnings about climate and ecological collapse have gone largely unanswered by governments, media and markets.
snippet: From scientists to intelligence agencies, repeated warnings about climate and ecological collapse have gone largely unanswered by governments, media and markets.
creator: Rupert Read

title: Prairie Prophecy: A powerful new film on Wes Jackson and the future of agriculture
content: A new documentary, Prairie Prophecy, explores the life and work of ecologist Wes Jackson, offering a compelling vision for the future of agriculture, sustainability, and our relationship with the land.

title: The restorative promise of agroecology: Farming for sovereignty and resilience in Malawi
content: In Malawi, where agriculture is central to daily life, an internship with an agroecology organisation offers an inside look at farming practices that challenge industrial food systems and support food sovereignty.

title: A New Wave of Collaboration for Our Oceans
content: In January, we launched a brand-new Marine Ecosystems Community of Practice – a collaborative initiative bringing together four Ecosystem Restoration Communities (ERCs) working at the frontlines of coastal and marine restoration.

title: The Ag Coop that Shares More than Machinery
content: What if, instead of going into debt to invest in their farms, farmers came together to pool equipment? What if, instead of struggling to run a small farm alone, there was extra help at hand? What if new entrants could draw on the experience of more established farmers in their local area? It may sound too good to be true, but this is the reality with France’s network of Agricultural Machinery Cooperatives (CUMA).
